extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL Remco dominated a 70km opening TT with 20-second splits at first check, taking 43 seconds from ROGLIČ on a course averaging 53+ kph
tactical_patterns: UAE Emirates deployed dual TT threats McNulty and Vine strategically early to set psychological benchmarks before GC leaders.
Evenepoel's 40-second margin by climb base suggests pre-climb TT power separates WorldTour specialists more than climbing weight advantage.
Geoghegan Hart lost 7 seconds on flat but clawed back time on final climb, revealing climbing TT specialists excel on mixed-terrain formats.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: 20-second advantage at first intermediate destroyed field's morale before decisive final climb even began
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: 43-second deficit to Evenepoel despite reportedly clean ride suggests fundamental TT power gap, not just bad day
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: beating specialized TT engines Küng and Ganna while conceding only 29 seconds to Evenepoel marks him as podium threat
rider_signals.VINE Jay: positive: second-fastest time while recovering from spring knee injury reveals UAE's unexpected TT depth beyond McNulty
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: neutral: third place finish 10 seconds down indicates even specialist chronomen can't match Grand Tour contenders on climbing TTs
